title: Vegetable Lasagne

overview.description: Layers of roasted vegetables, tomato sauce, creamy béchamel and pasta baked with bubbling cheese.

method: Roast the courgettes, peppers and mushrooms at 220°C (200°C fan) for 25 minutes until lightly caramelised.
Meanwhile, heat the olive oil over a medium heat.
Cook the onions and carrots for 10 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the tomato purée and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped tomatoes, oregano and basil.
Stir in the roasted vegetables and spinach until the spinach has wilted.
Spread a little tomato sauce over the base of a baking dish.
Layer lasagne sheets, vegetable sauce and béchamel.
Repeat until all the ingredients have been used.
Finish with béchamel, mozzarella and Parmesan.
Bake at 200°C (180°C fan) for 40–45 minutes until golden and bubbling.

learning.did-you-know: Lasagne doesn't have to contain meat. Vegetable versions have long been popular throughout Italy, particularly when seasonal produce is abundant.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How roasting vegetables concentrates their flavour and prevents a watery lasagne.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A hearty vegetarian main that's perfect for batch cooking and uses vegetables already common in the Chapman Kitchen.
chapman-tips: Roasting the vegetables first removes excess moisture and concentrates their flavour.
Allow the lasagne to rest before cutting so the layers stay neat.
Homemade béchamel gives a noticeably better result.
This is an excellent way to use vegetables that are starting to soften in the fridge.
notes.batch-cooking: Bake, cool completely and freeze whole or in individual portions for up to 6 months.
notes.leftovers: Reheats beautifully in the oven or microwave.
